title: Gaultheria minutiflora
taxonomy: Gaultheria minutiflora is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. Gaultheria minutiflora is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub that typically reaches a height of 10-30 centimeters. Gaultheria minutiflora produces small, white flowers in late spring and red berries in fall. Gaultheria minutiflora is closely related to other Gaultheria species, which are commonly known as wintergreen or checkerberry plants.

intro: Gaultheria minutiflora, also known as dwarf mountain tea or small-flowered Gaultheria, is a small, shrubby plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Gaultheria minutiflora contains a range of bioactive constituents, including phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Gaultheria minutiflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
